summary: PR 2525 decompiled substantial player bonus logic in src/melee/pl/plbonus.c, replacing the fn_80039618 asm placeholder with C that computes many bonus flags from player action stats, stale move table data, game mode helpers, and threshold fields. It also adjusted fn_80038700 with a tightly scoped dont_inline pragma, changed digit arithmetic in fn_8003CC84, and typed several previously opaque pl_804D6470_t fields in src/melee/pl/types.h. The only discussion evidence is an automated progress report showing fn_80039618 improved from 0.00% to 99.67%, fn_8003CC84 reached 100%, and plbonus .data/.sdata2 reached 100%.

key_files.role: Main decomp target; adds C body for fn_80039618, scopes dont_inline around fn_80038700, and changes arithmetic in fn_8003CC84.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/pl/plbonus.c added 319 deleted 3 hunks 4
raw_diff: replaces /// #fn_80039618 with a full C implementation
raw_diff: adds #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on before fn_80038700 and #pragma pop after it
raw_diff: fn_8003CC84 changes digit extraction from modulo expressions to subtraction-based arithmetic
text_corpus: bot report lists main/melee/pl/plbonus fn_80039618 +6678 bytes, 0.00% to 99.67%; fn_8003CC84 +34 bytes, 97.94% to 100.00%

key_files.role: Header/type support; recovers typed fields in pl_804D6470_t used by plbonus thresholds.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/pl/types.h added 9 deleted 6 hunks 4
raw_diff: pl_804D6470_t byte arrays at x0, x8, x20, x50, xF0, and x160 are replaced or split into float/u32/int fields

what_changed.text: Implemented fn_80039618 in C with many player bonus flag checks based on plActionStats, pl_StaleMoveTableExt_t, Player_Get* helpers, gm_* helpers, and pl_804D6470 threshold fields.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80039618 body added
text_corpus: bot report fn_80039618 0.00% to 99.67%

what_changed.text: Added a scoped #pragma dont_inline around fn_80038700, a small summation helper used by fn_80039618.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on before fn_80038700; #pragma pop after fn_80038700
raw_diff: fn_80039618 calls fn_80038700 for sum_attacks and sum_x358

what_changed.text: Changed fn_8003CC84 digit extraction to compute tens and ones by subtracting hundreds/tens products rather than using chained modulo expressions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: temp_r3_3 and temp_r0 assignments changed in fn_8003CC84
text_corpus: bot report fn_8003CC84 97.94% to 100.00%

what_changed.text: Recovered typed pl_804D6470_t fields at several offsets, including floats x0, x8, x10, x20, x160 and u32/int threshold fields xC, x50, x54, xF0.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: src/melee/pl/types.h pl_804D6470_t field changes

smart_moves.text: The large function uses local aliases for repeated structures and counters, such as table, stats, gobj, hit_counts, atk_counts, x358_counts, and totals, reducing repeated nested access while preserving offset-style field names where semantics are not proven.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: first block of fn_80039618 declares table/stats/gobj and cached stats fields

smart_moves.text: Threshold fields in pl_804D6470_t were typed instead of leaving byte-array padding, allowing fn_80039618 to compare against real float/u32 fields rather than raw offset math.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pl_804D6470_t byte arrays changed to float/u32 fields

smart_moves.text: Repeated action-count checks are expressed with loops over index ranges and a grouped switch for selected attack IDs, rather than duplicating each case as separate blocks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: loops over i=1..99, i=0x40..0x62, j=0..5, k ranges; switch cases 0x42/0x46/0x4A/0x4E/0x56

decomp_lessons.lesson: For player-stat bonus logic, recover enough of the stats and threshold structs to express comparisons through typed fields before attempting the large function body.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80039618 uses plActionStats and pl_804D6470_t fields throughout
raw_diff: pl_804D6470_t fields typed in types.h

decomp_lessons.lesson: Natural loops can represent many repeated bonus checks over action index ranges; this PR uses loops for hit counts, stale-move/action ranges, player slots, and opponent KOs.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80039618 loops over hit_counts, x358_counts, k action ranges, player slots, and KOs

decomp_lessons.lesson: Grouped switch cases are useful for selected action-ID subsets inside a broader loop.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: switch over i from 0x40 to 0x62 accumulates sub_sum for cases 0x42, 0x46, 0x4A, 0x4E, 0x56

naming_conventions.text: Unrecovered global/function names remain address-style, such as fn_80039618, fn_80038700, pl_80038628, and pl_804D6470, while clear local roles use short semantic names like table, stats, gobj, hit_counts, atk_counts, x358_counts, found_low, found_high, max_ratio, and sum_ratio.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80039618 declarations and calls

naming_conventions.text: Newly typed struct members in pl_804D6470_t keep offset-style xNN names rather than speculative semantic names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pl_804D6470_t fields x0, x8, xC, x10, x20, x50, x54, xF0, x160
raw_diff: #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around fn_80038700

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR 2525: typed threshold fields supported a large plbonus function decomp
accepted_candidate_records.text: In plbonus, recovering typed pl_804D6470_t threshold fields in types.h accompanied the C implementation of fn_80039618, allowing comparisons against fields such as x0, x8, xC, x10, x20, x50, x54, xF0, and x160 rather than leaving those ranges as byte arrays.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pl_804D6470_t field typing
raw_diff: fn_80039618 uses those fields in threshold checks

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR 2525: loop and switch structure in fn_80039618
accepted_candidate_records.text: The fn_80039618 implementation expresses repeated player-stat and action-range checks with loops over counts/player slots and a grouped switch for selected IDs, matching the natural-loop guidance in the loaded standards.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: loops and grouped switch in fn_80039618
decomp_standards:natural-loops
decomp_standards:canonical-control-flow-and-macros

source_update_candidates.title: plbonus.c owns player bonus/stat flag calculation work
source_update_candidates.text: src/melee/pl/plbonus.c contains player bonus calculation logic including fn_80039618, which sets many bonus flags from plActionStats, stale move table fields, player/entity helpers, and game-mode helpers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80039618 implementation
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful searchable path fact for future plbonus/player-stat decomp work; should be curated as source-owned path metadata, not promoted directly by intake.

source_update_candidates.title: Record rationale when using scoped dont_inline or PAD_STACK in large decomp functions
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2525 used a tightly scoped dont_inline pragma around fn_80038700 and PAD_STACK(16) inside fn_80039618. Existing standards already treat pragmas and stack padding as exceptional tactics; curator may consider adding this PR as an example requiring explicit verifier notes rather than as a new accepted style.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: scoped dont_inline around fn_80038700
raw_diff: PAD_STACK(16) in fn_80039618
decomp_standards:matching-tactics-need-evidence
decomp_standards:avoid-pragmas-register-asm
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR is a concrete historical example of exceptional tactics, but loaded evidence lacks a rationale; route to curator as a possible standards example, not an accepted new rule.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that dont_inline or PAD_STACK are generally acceptable from this PR alone; the loaded evidence lacks explicit objdiff rationale and adjacent regression checks.
Do not promote semantic names for pl_804D6470_t xNN fields; the PR only supports typed offset-style fields, not meanings.
Do not treat the hsd_3AA7 automated report improvements as changed-file facts for this PR slice; no baselib file changes are loaded.
Do not claim fn_80039618 fully matched; the automated report says 99.67%, not 100.00%.
